Reentrancy.description: Reentrancy vulnerability allows external calls to re-enter the contract before state changes are completed

Timestamp Dependency.description: Block timestamp dependency can be manipulated by miners

Integer Overflow/Underflow.description: Integer overflow/underflow in arithmetic operations (Note: Fixed in Solidity 0.8.0+)

Dangerous Delegatecall.description: Delegatecall to untrusted contract can lead to unexpected behavior

Transaction-Ordering Dependence.description: Transaction ordering can change contract outcomes and enable front-running

Uninitialized Storage Pointer.description: Uninitialized local storage pointers can alias unexpected storage slots

Unchecked External Calls.description: Low-level external call results are ignored instead of being checked

components.TimestampInvoc: Function invokes timestamp-related variables (now, block.timestamp, block.number)
components.TimestampAssign: Function assigns timestamp values to variables
components.TimestampContaminate: Timestamp is used in conditional operations or logic

Transaction-Ordering Dependence.rule: Public/external function manipulates shared order-sensitive state and uses competitive logic or value flow

components.shared_state: Variables such as bids, price, orders, balances, allowance, winner, nonce, or pending state are read/written
components.competitive_logic: Comparisons or value transfers suggest outcomes may depend on transaction ordering

Uninitialized Storage Pointer.rule: Local storage variable is declared without initialization

components.storage_pointer: Pattern like `Type storage name;` appears inside the function body

Unchecked External Calls.rule: Low-level external call is made and its return value is not checked

components.unchecked_result: The result is not wrapped in require/assert/if and is not assigned to a boolean
